title: My Family
summary: A man describes his family members, what they look like, and what they like to do.
body: I have a small family. There are four people in my family. My wife is called Sarah. She has long brown hair and kind eyes. She works as a nurse at the local hospital. My son is called Tom. He is eight years old. He loves playing football with his friends. My daughter is called Emma. She is five years old. She likes drawing pictures and playing with her cat. Our cat is called Biscuit. He is orange and very fluffy. We all live in a small house near a park. At the weekend, we often go to the park together. Tom plays football there and Emma feeds the ducks. Sarah and I sit on a bench and watch them. In the evenings, we eat dinner together as a family. After dinner, Tom does his homework. Emma listens to stories before bed. Sarah reads a book and I watch the news. We are a happy family.

annotations.exampleEn: She has long brown hair that reaches her shoulders.
Everyone trusted her because she had kind eyes.
The nurse checked the patient's temperature.
My mother works at the local hospital.
The fluffy cat sat on the warm cushion.
We usually visit grandma at the weekend.
The children love to feed the ducks at the pond.
They sat on a bench in the park and talked.
We try to eat dinner together as a family every evening.
She listens to stories on her tablet before bed.
My father likes to watch the news every evening.
We often go for a walk after dinner.

grammarNotes.title: Simple present for habits and routines
grammarNotes.bodyVi: Simple present dùng để mô tả các thói quen và hành động lặp đi lặp lại: 'Tom loves playing football.' / 'We often go to the park together.' Với he/she/it, thêm '-s' hoặc '-es' vào động từ.
grammarNotes.bodyEn: The simple present describes habits and repeated actions: 'Tom loves playing football.' / 'We often go to the park together.' With he/she/it, add '-s' or '-es' to the verb.

grammarNotes.title: Possessive adjectives
grammarNotes.bodyVi: Tính từ sở hữu (my, his, her, our, their) đứng trước danh từ để chỉ sự sở hữu: 'My wife is called Sarah.' / 'Her cat is called Biscuit.' / 'Our house is near a park.'
grammarNotes.bodyEn: Possessive adjectives (my, his, her, our, their) come before nouns to show ownership: 'My wife is called Sarah.' / 'Her cat is called Biscuit.' / 'Our house is near a park.'

translationVi: Tôi có một gia đình nhỏ. Có bốn người trong gia đình tôi. Vợ tôi tên là Sarah. Cô ấy có mái tóc nâu dài và đôi mắt hiền lành. Cô ấy làm y tá tại bệnh viện địa phương. Con trai tôi tên là Tom. Em ấy tám tuổi. Em ấy rất thích đá bóng với các bạn. Con gái tôi tên là Emma. Em ấy năm tuổi. Em ấy thích vẽ tranh và chơi với con mèo của mình. Con mèo của chúng tôi tên là Biscuit. Nó màu cam và rất bông xù. Tất cả chúng tôi sống trong một ngôi nhà nhỏ gần công viên. Vào cuối tuần, chúng tôi thường đến công viên cùng nhau. Tom chơi bóng đá ở đó và Emma cho vịt ăn. Sarah và tôi ngồi trên ghế và nhìn các con. Buổi tối, chúng tôi ăn tối cùng nhau như một gia đình. Sau bữa tối, Tom làm bài tập về nhà. Emma nghe kể chuyện trước khi đi ngủ. Sarah đọc sách và tôi xem tin tức. Chúng tôi là một gia đình hạnh phúc.

questions.prompt: How many people are in the narrator's family?
questions.explanation: 'There are four people in my family.'

questions.prompt: What does the narrator's wife look like?
options: Short black hair and blue eyes
Long brown hair and kind eyes
Curly red hair and green eyes
Straight blonde hair and brown eyes
questions.explanation: 'She has long brown hair and kind eyes.'
'She works as a nurse at the local hospital.'

questions.hint: Find the sentence about Tom's age.

questions.explanation: 'He loves playing football with his friends.'

questions.prompt: What is the family cat's name?
questions.hint: Find the sentence about the cat.
Look for the description of the cat.

questions.prompt: Where does the family often go at the weekend?
questions.explanation: 'We often go to the park together.'
questions.hint: Find the sentence about weekend activities.

questions.prompt: What does Emma do at the park?
questions.hint: Find the sentence about what Emma does at the park.

questions.prompt: What do Sarah and the narrator do at the park?
options: They sit on a bench and watch
questions.explanation: 'Sarah and I sit on a bench and watch them.'
questions.hint: Look for what the parents do at the park.

questions.prompt: What does Emma do before bed?
questions.hint: Find the sentence about Emma's bedtime routine.

questions.prompt: What does the narrator do after dinner?
questions.hint: Look at the evening routine sentences.

'She works as a nurse at the local hospital.'
'He loves playing football with his friends.'
'we eat dinner together as a family'

criticalThinkingQuestion: The narrator says his family is happy. What do you think makes a family happy? What activities do you enjoy doing together with your family?
